title: Izzy the Kiddo and the Crystal Mountain Rescue
summary: A brave family of moles—Papa Mole, Disco, Thistle, and Baby Mole—embarks on a treacherous climb up the Magical Crystal Mountain to rescue Dingo, a fellow mole stranded at the peak with no boots. Aided by Izzy the Kiddo, the group survives wibbly-wobbly crystal bridges, a multi-colored glowing tunnel, and a encounter with a giant fluffy bunny. Armed with superhero costumes, flashlights, and a fresh pair of orangey-yellow galoshes, the team reaches the summit to save their friend. After a hearty meal of ants and worms, the moles complete their mission and return home safely as heroes.

highlightQuote.text: I can be brave by giving you some superhero costumes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story about Crystal Mountain, Crystal Mountain, Crystal...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, we got my watermelon?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Watermelon?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And honeydew.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Why?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I miss eating those.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they are delicious.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need to buy them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Do you think the moles packed those in their provisions for their journey?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mr. Moles, did you pack watermelon and honeydew?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You know we did, we packed some, some melon.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The moles packed watermelon and honeydew.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what else did they pack? We decided earlier, they packed...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They packed worms and ants!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well yes, lots of good mole snacks because it's going to be a hard journey up to the top of the mountain to do their rescue. And what else? Warm jackets, and boots. Now what was special about the boots that they're bringing?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they realized they need to bring an extra pair of boots because the mole who's stranded at the top, who seeker asked them to rescue, is up there and couldn't get down because his boots fell apart. So they need to bring extra boots.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I have those galoshes boots! I have those galoshed boots in my pocket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they have big backpacks. Disco, Thistle, and Papa Mole are wearing big backpacks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I got those orangey-yellow boots. Is that friend that Seeker was talking about had orangey-yellow boots?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He would like some, so we better bring those for him. And... just going to set the blue timer. Okay. And now... oh and we've got our cold weather gear and our tents...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mr. Mole, this is your house. So this is going to be your house. So please leave your house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, so we're leaving our house to go on our expedition. And so they landed their airplane at the base of the magical Crystal Mountain and started their trek up the mountain. And who went first? Papa Mole, or Disco, or Thistle, or Baby Mole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Everybody!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they all went together.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Holding hands.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, holding hands. And it was a beautiful bright sunny day as they began their ascent. Ascent means going up. Up the side of the Crystal Mountain. And the first part of it that they climbed was a beautiful purple crystal. And it had stairs cut into the side, and so it was easy for them to climb. Climb, climb, climb, climb, climb. And they climbed all morning until they reached a plateau. That's a flat part. And Disco said, "Excuse me, can we pause for a snack?" And Papa Mole said, "Okay, now's a good time for a snack." And so they put out a blanket and put down their packs, and they sat down for a little snack. And Papa Mole said, "But not for too long, we've got to keep going if we're going to make it to the top." And they looked around...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then they hurried to eat their snacks and they ate them quicky-quick and then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then they kept on going. And Papa Mole said, "Aha, up ahead is our first challenge. Do you see that? At the edge of this plateau, before we go on, we have to cross a big crack in the mountain." There's a big empty space and it goes very deep into the mountain where it cracked open. But there is a bridge across, a crystal bridge, but it is a wibbly-wobbly bridge.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can be brave by giving you some superhero costumes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Superhero costumes?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So you can run across the bridge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco says, "Yeah!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here you go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. What's on my superhero costume?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some gloves, special gloves, a cape, special superhero outfit, and a helmet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! And Disco pulled on the gloves and the outfit and the helmet, and he ran across the wibbly-wobbly bridge without being afraid at all. And it wibbled and wobbled, and it creaked and it rocked, but he made it across safely. And he said, "Okay everybody, you can do it too!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Papa Mole carried Baby Mole and he held Thistle's hand, and they ran across the creaky bridge as well. And that way they made it across the dangerous crevasse, the crack in the side of the Crystal Mountain. And on this side, instead of purple, the crystal was pink. And it was a little bit slippery, but there was a path. And so they walked up the path carefully.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Zigging and zagging back and forth across the face of the mountain. And it was still bright and sunny out, but it started to get a little bit cold and windy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Did blow any other superhero things off?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What did you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nip, did blow any other superhero things off?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, did it blow their superhero things off? Oh, good question.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "Oh no! One of my gloves got blown off by the wind. What are we going to do?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I got it! I got it with my neck!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good catch with your neck! Thanks, Izzy. And he put it back on. "I'm so glad I have this superhero costume," said Disco. "It helps me feel brave." And Thistle says, "Yeah, you can lead us up this next part. Oh, what's that?" And Papa Mole said, "Huh, there's a dark circle on the side of the mountain. It looks like a tunnel." And in fact...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right above it, there's a really steep part of the mountain. So I bet that tunnel is a shortcut. Maybe if we go through the tunnel, we'll come out the other side higher up. And Disco said, "Yeah, let's try it!" But wait a minute... and he poked his head into the mouth of the tunnel. "It's so dark in here, I'm a little bit scared."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I pressed the button of my, and then I pressed the button of my flashlight and it lit up the tunnel.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks, Izzy! Wow, now I can see. And the tunnel sparkled beautifully in all different colors. Because as it passed through the inside of the Crystal Mountain, the crystal all around the edges of the tunnel was red and orange and yellow and green and purple. And the moles went through one by one, single file, one at a time because it was a narrow tunnel. It was only wide enough for one mole at a time to go through. And so they held onto a rope so they wouldn't get separated as they went through the tunnel. And their mole fur glowed in different colors as the sun passed through the crystal and lit them up. And finally, as they carefully walked out to the end, Disco popped his head out into the air and said, "Wow, everybody, come on out!" And they all came out the other end of the tunnel and looked around. And now they were so high up in the mountain, on the side of the mountain, that they could see for miles all around. They could see clouds and forests and other mountains below them. And Papa Mole looked up and said, "My goodness, we have a lot further to go. I wonder, since the sun is starting to set, maybe we should camp here for the night and finish our rescue tomorrow. What do you think, Moles?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Great!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Great!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Great? Okay. And so Papa Mole pitched the tent. He set up the tent for them to sleep in. And then they set up a blanket and they sat around and had their dinner. What did they eat for dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ants and worms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ants and worms, yum. And then they all went to sleep, zzzz, safe in their tent. And then they woke up the next morning and it was time to make their way to the very top of the mountain to rescue the poor little mole who was stuck up there. And Papa Mole said, "Hmm, I think I see a way to the top. Over this purple part..."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "...and finally onto the rocky orange crystal that will take us to the very top. But there's something there, right at the edge of the orange crystal, that looks like it's in the way of the path. Let's get going."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Block!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's blocking us. Actually, actually I got it and I put it in my pocket, and the other pocket. And I got out the galoshes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was it that you put in your pocket?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was the stone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, there was a stone blocking the path. And I put it in my left pocket, and I got out the galoshes. Oh good, and now you have a place to go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Excellent. So the path was unblocked. And so Papa Mole and Disco and Thistle and Baby Mole made their way up the path over the rocky purple crystal, and zigging and zagging back and forth up and down the rocks. And finally they made it to the edge of the orange crystal, which wasn't blocked anymore. And as they continued their way, just as they got close to the top of the Crystal Mountain, there was something much bigger blocking their way. They...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I moved it and put it in the... I got a piece of bark and I put it in my pocket, so it won't be in the way.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but this was so big it didn't even fit in your pocket. It was huge and round, and as fluffy as a pillow, and as furry as a bunny rabbit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then I sent it down... with the rock and this... with the rock and the piece of bark. I sent it down to the bottom and cleared it so no one would step on it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that was a very nice thing of you to do.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But as you got closer to this huge white fluffy thing, two little ears popped out the top, and two little eyes popped open. And it said, "Oh! Who are you?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, we got very great news.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, it's we're rescuing a mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "A mole? And you yourselves, you appear to be moles, is that correct?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but I'm not a mole, I'm Izzy the kiddo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, hello Izzy the kiddo! Well, welcome to my mountain, the Mountain of Crystal Magic. Uh, where are you headed now?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm headed to the mole. Oh, I see. And he lost his hiking galoshes, so I got orangey-yellow ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Well, that is an excellent mission that you are on. I see I appear to be in your way because my very large fluffy body is blocking the path. What can we do about that?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's that mean? Oh, the timer blinked, so it's almost time to finish our story. "Hmm, perhaps I'll just hop right over you, and then you'll be on the other side and you can continue up to the top. What do you think?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's count to three and I'll do a big hop. One, two, three, boing! And the big fluffy bunny bounced right over the moles and was no longer in the way. And Papa Mole said, "Oh, I think I see the mole who is stranded. What was his name again?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Slingo Bingo Bunga Dinga.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about I could call him Dingo for short.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dingo, okay. So Dingo the mole was up at the top of the mountain, looking very lonely and sad and cold. And you know what? His feet, his little paws were bare, because his boots were all fallen apart. And so Papa Mole rushed up and said, "Come on Disco, come on Thistle, come on Baby Mole!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, come on Dingo, come on, I got some new orangey and yellow galoshes for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh thank you! My feet hurt so much because my boots fell apart. Let's try them on." Then he popped them on his feet and they said, "They fit perfectly. Thank you for rescuing me. And I've been up here for days and I'm so hungry and thirsty. Do you have anything for me?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, we got worms and ants.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Worm and ants, delicious. Am-nam-nam-nam-nam. And he gobbled up his snack and a little bit of water and he said, "Now, let's go back down so I can go home." And so the moles trekked right back down the mountain safely, and they were able to take their new friend Dingo back home, and he was so tired he fell right to sleep. And that's the end of our story.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Thistle
characters.description: A spirited mole child navigating the biting winds and frosted peaks, one of the small, adventurous souls braving the treacherous snowy climb.

characters.name: Baby Mole
characters.description: The youngest mole, once carried by kin through a grueling mountain ascent to unearth a shimmering artifact of ancient magic, survived the world-scorching fires of a volcanic eruption by sheltering deep within the sanctuary of the burrow.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Slingo Bingo Bunga Dinga
characters.description: The stranded mole at the summit, also known as 'Dingo' for short.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: The Big Fluffy Bunny
characters.description: The fluffy guardian of the Mountain of Crystal Magic.

places.name: Magical Crystal Mountain
places.description: A peak made of vibrant, multi-colored crystals with stairs and tunnels.

places.name: The Moles' House
places.description: The starting point of the moles' expedition.

places.name: The Plateau
places.description: A flat section of the mountain where the group stops for a snack.

places.name: The Purple Crystal
places.description: The first section of the climb, featuring stairs cut into the side.

places.name: The Crystal Bridge
places.description: A wibbly-wobbly bridge spanning a deep crack in the mountain.

places.name: The Sparkly Tunnel
places.description: A narrow shortcut through the mountain lined with glowing, colorful crystals.

places.name: The Mountain of Crystal Magic
places.description: The higher reaches of the mountain where the Big Fluffy Bunny resides.
